LAW AND MOTION, 302, DEFENDANTS EOIN O'TOOLE, AND, K2 PARTNERING SOLUTIONS WEST, INC. AMENDED DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. NO APPEARANCES ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: OFF CALENDAR, AS MOOT. FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT FILED. JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE CSR #5797

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT EOIN O'TOOLE DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DEMURRER IS OVERRULED, 10 DAYS LEAVE TO ANSWER. 3 WITKEN, SUMMARY OF CALIFORNIA LAW, AGENCY, SEC. 199 P. 252 PROVIDES THAT "AN AGENT OR EMPLOYEE IS ALWAYS LIABLE FOR HIS OR HER OWN TORTS, WHETHER THE PRINCIPAL IS LIABLE OR NOT, IN SPITE OF THE FACT THAT THE AGENT ACTS IN ACCORDANCE WITH THE PRINCIPAL'S DIRECTIONS.... SIMILARLY, AN AGENT WHO COMMITS AN INDEPENDENT TORT, SUCH AS FRAUD, REMAINS LIABLE DESPITE THE FACT THAT THE PRINCIPAL, BY RATIFICATION, ALSO BECOMES LIABLE." THE EMPLOYEE IS LIABLE, BUT MAY OBTAIN INDEMNIFICATION UNDER LABOR CODE SECTION 2802 AND PRINCIPLES OF AGENCY/RESPONDEAT SUPERIOR. THE PLAIN READING OF LABOR CODE SECTIONS 970 AND 972 INDICATES THAT O'TOOLE CAN BE LIABLE FOR MISREPRESENTATIONS AND NO CLEAR CASE AUTHORITY REFUTES THIS. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jun-24-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT EOIN O'TOOLE DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Appearances: Joseph A. Creitz, P.C. Attorney at Law, (415) 269-3675 appearing for Plaintiff. The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Demurrer is overruled, 10 days leave to answer. 3 Witken, Summary of California Law, Agency, sec. 199 p. 252 provides that "an agent or employee is always liable for his or her own torts, whether the principal is liable or not, in spite of the fact that the agent acts in accordance with the principal's directions.... Similarly, an agent who commits an independent tort, such as fraud, remains liable despite the fact that the principal, by ratification, also becomes liable." The employee is liable, but may obtain indemnification under Labor Code section 2802 and principles of agency/respondeat superior. The plain reading of Labor Code sections 970 and 972 indicates that O'Toole can be liable for misrepresentations and no clear case authority refutes this. Order signed in open Court.(302/CWW)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine, CSR #5797
